jobFit.explanation: 6 of 12 hard requirements have direct resume bullet evidence, 5 have keyword-only evidence, 1 have no deterministic evidence. Strengthen real bullet evidence for: automation, communication, documentation, javascript, workflow. Confirm whether these reflect real experience before claiming them: stakeholder. This deterministic fit signal reflects keyword/bullet overlap only, not a hiring decision.

items.suggested: - [rephrase: worked on ->] platform support and release coordination for internal operations teams; replace the marked filler with a concrete, truthful action and add scope/result if available.

items.why: The bullet does not start with a recognized action verb, so the contribution may be harder to scan.
evidenceRequired: Which action did you personally take: built, led, improved, shipped, reduced, migrated, supported, or similar?
Keep the original claim truthful; do not add responsibilities that are not in your source resume or notes.

items.why: The bullet has no deterministic numeric evidence for scale, effort, or outcome.
evidenceRequired: Is there a truthful number for users, volume, time saved, cost, revenue, reliability, frequency, team size, or duration?
If no metric exists, keep the bullet qualitative rather than inventing a number.

issues.message: Job posting terms not found in resume: analyst, build, communication, improve, lightweight, partner, readiness, stakeholder, tools, workflow, write.
issues.recommendation: Only add missing terms when they truthfully describe real experience or relevant projects.

checklist: Confirm every suggested edit is truthful and backed by real experience.
Map resume claims to the target posting without inventing skills, dates, employers, or credentials.
Prefer accomplishment bullets with action, scope, and result.
Keep formatting simple: clear headings, consistent dates, and readable bullets.
Save a reviewed copy before sending it to an employer.

items.item: Every content change is backed by source resume evidence, user-confirmed notes, or a clearly marked question.
No unsupported metrics, tools, titles, employers, dates, credentials, locations, or work authorization claims were added.
Each job-specific edit maps to real resume evidence or is left as a user question instead of a claim.
Headings, dates, bullets, tense, and punctuation are consistent enough for human and ATS scanning.
Private drafts, source files, and run outputs are kept out of git and shared locations unless explicitly sanitized.
